Welcome back to The Long View. I'm joined by Skander Armanath, who reads the macroeconomy for a living. Skander, good to have you. Thanks, Alex. Today's episode is sponsored by Bloomberg. Bloomberg Terminal is the data spine institutional desks run on. Try Bloomberg dot com today. You described macro as risk management. Unpack that. Macro is risk management at scale. The job is mostly not being on the wrong side of a regime change and the regime change you can name in advance is the one you can position for. The rest is just hoping. And the economy is a system, not a set of levers. Pull one and three others move. When we backtested the calls, the misses were always someone treating a correlated system as if the parts were independent. So the same discipline Eleanor talks about in sustainability. Exactly the same. A macrobook and a power grid are both systems of correlated risk. Systems, thinking, and risk management are one discipline you just changed the vocabulary. Thanks again to Morningstar for backing the show. Morningstar gives you independent fund ratings. Thanks, Alex. That's it for this Long View. Take care.
